{
    "cefr_level": "B1",

        "look to life simplie -> look at life more simply (Grammar/Spelling)",
        "In futcher -> In the future (Spelling/Grammar)",
        "networking give you a lot opportunity -> networking gives you a lot of opportunities (Grammar)",
        "need count -> need to count (Grammar)",
        "think what you study -> think that you study (Grammar)",
        "hasn't free time -> don't have free time (Grammar)",
        "try note your day -> try noting your day / try to keep a log of your day (Grammar)",
        "expirience -> experience (Spelling)",
        "habbits what take realy a lot time -> habits that take really a lot of time (Spelling/Grammar)",
        "Delite them -> Delete them (Spelling)",

        "unlacky -> unlucky (Spelling)"
    ],

While there are frequent spelling and grammar errors, the student successfully expresses complex ideas, provides structured advice, and uses a variety of discourse markers ('Firstly', 'Secondly', 'Also'). The vocabulary is ambitious (e.g., 'networking', 'time management', 'procrastination' implied by 'bad habits'), and the meaning is clear throughout despite the mistakes. The text follows a logical organization and achieves the communicative goal of the task."
}
